jobs.title: Finance Manager - FP&A & Controlling
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Dust

Work is being rewritten, and the people holding the pen are the ones who actually run it.

Dust is the multiplayer AI platform for human-agent collaboration. It gives companies a shared workspace where teams can build, deploy, and manage AI agents connected to their company knowledge, tools, and workflows. With enterprise-grade governance, flexible model choice, and a collaborative interface for humans and agents to work together, Dust empowers AI Operators at the world’s fastest-moving companies to rewire how work gets done

With 70%+ weekly active users, people stick with Dust as much as they do with Slack and Notion. We don't get piloted and shelved. We land once, and spread. We're at an exciting stage of our journey, and growing fast.

We're serving great customers like Datadog, 1Password, Cursor, Clay, Vanta and Persona, and aim to x5 our growth by the end of 2026.

Dust is backed by Sequoia with a determined team of optimists (coming from Stripe, OpenAI, and Stanford) who like to focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously while doing so. The Generalist named us among the Future 50.

What You'll Do

This hire is the first dedicated FP&A and Controlling resource at Dust. The mandate is to build the financial backbone the business needs to scale: from clean accounting oversight to forward-looking models, all supercharged by AI. This is not a role where you maintain someone else's work. You build it and reinvent it along the way..

 

The role sits across three pillars, each roughly equal in time and importance:

1. Accounting Oversight & Controlling (~1/3)

You are not the accountant booking the entries. But you own the quality of the numbers.

  • Drive the month-end close process: partner with our external accounting firms (France & US) to ensure the monthly close is clean, on time, and decision-grade, challenge entries, review accruals, ensure period accuracy

  • Own the accounting-to-reporting bridge: translate statutory P&L into the management reporting. Build and enforce the chart of accounts and analytical mapping to make reporting reliable and scalable

  • Flag risks proactively: revenue recognition edge cases, deferred revenue, capitalized development costs. You stand over the logic to make sure we translate the business into trustworthy accounting inputs and financial insights

2. FP&A - Model Architecture & Business Partnering (~1/3)

You are among the first persons to build FP&A at Dust. That means architecture decisions matter as much as outputs.

  • Design and model the integrated financial model: P&L, cash flow, headcount planning, unit economics and metrics

  • Build the annual budget process and participate to the quarterly forecast cycle and business reviews

  • Support strategic analysis as needed: scenario modeling, unit economics, fundraising datarooms

 

3. AI & Automation for Finance - Dust4Dust (~1/3)

It is a core part of the job. Dust transforms how work gets done and we are our first users. You will be expected to build, test, and deploy Finance agents on the Dust platform.

  • Identify repetitive Finance workflows and redesign them with AI: AP and AR automations, reconciliations, variance commentary, reporting drafts, data validation, … Uses cases are endless

  • Develop real convictions on what AI-augmented Finance looks like in practice

  • Act as a pilot user and internal advocate for Dust's product, generating feedback loops between Finance and Product

 

What We're Looking For

Experience and background:

  • 5-7 years in Finance, with a clear arc from accounting-trained foundations toward FP&A and finance

  • Exposure to both close/controlling work and forward-looking planning - you have done both and value both

  • AI/SaaS company background is a plus - you understand ARR, churn, CAC, and why revenue recognition in software is not trivial

  • Experience in a scaling environment where processes did not exist yet and you had to build them

 

Technical skills:

  • Strong financial modeling: you build from scratch, not from templates

  • Data-literate: comfortable with SQL or willing to learn, experienced with Data & BI tools (Metabase, Snowflake, or equivalent)

Mindset:

  • Automation-obsessed: your default reaction to a recurring task is "how do I make this run itself"

  • Builder mentality: energized by the blank page, not intimidated by it

  • Communication: you can explain a P&L variance to a non-Finance person and knows what they need to see versus what stays in the model

  • Business enabler: You see Finance as a function that makes the business move faster and smarter - not one that slows it down with process for process's sake. You earn trust with business leaders by being the person who shows up with options, while never losing sight of the controls and rigor that protect the company long-term

  • Intellectual curiosity about AI: you don't need to be a technologist or an engineer, but you need genuine interest

 

Why Join

  • Build from scratch: the Finance function at Dust is being designed now. Your fingerprints will be on the architecture for years.

  • AI-first by default: you will use and shape the product you work for, every day.

  • Scale trajectory: Dust is growing fast. This role grows with it, both in scope and seniority.

Location

We're prioritizing building our team with an in-person culture at our offices in Paris, San Francisco, and New York because we value the magic that happens when talented people work closely together.

We have an office-first culture. Some of the best things about building at Dust are the energy, the fast decisions, and the unexpected conversations that unlock a hard problem, which happen because we are in the same room. Being together is not a formality, it is how we do our best work, and it is something we actively protect.

That said, we hire people with strong judgement and we extend that trust to how they manage their time. When working from home makes more sense for what you need to get done that day, we trust you to make that call.

Why Dust

The models are powerful enough. What's missing is the product layer where AI meets how companies actually work. That's what we're building: the infrastructure that lets any team turn scattered knowledge and tools into coordinated execution with agents they build, own, and run themselves.

We use Dust ourselves every day. We get to shape how humans and agents collaborate while solving our own problems with the product we ship. That loop is rare, and it's why we move fast.

If you're excited about defining a new category and want to join a determined team of optimists who focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously, we'd love to talk.

Summary

We're looking for a Founder's Associate to work directly with our co-founder Gabriel for a 6-month internship (July 2026 - January 2027). You'll own the operational rhythm that keeps our leadership team aligned and our investors informed, from board materials to investor updates to teamOS.

This is a rare opportunity to see how a fast-growing, Sequoia-backed startup operates from the inside, work alongside founders daily, and build leverage across every part of the company. If you thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and want real responsibility from day one, this role will accelerate your career.

What you'll do

Executive Support & Operations

  • Own the preparation and delivery of monthly investor updates, ensuring they're accurate, compelling, and shipped on time

  • Manage board meeting preparation, coordinating inputs across functions and ensuring materials are polished and ready

  • Support the Chief of Staff on executive-level projects and strategic initiatives that have already been scoped and shipped

Team Operating System

  • Run weekly team meetings (weeklies), ensuring they're well-structured, actionable, and maintain team rhythm

  • Maintain and improve teamOS—our internal systems for goal-setting, accountability, and cross-functional coordination

  • Partner with Area Leads and Ops to keep the team drumbeat consistent and effective

Stakeholder Coordination

  • Act as a trusted liaison between the Chief of Staff, founders (especially Gabriel), and cross-functional partners

  • Ensure nothing falls through the cracks during a critical period when the Chief of Staff is on leave

  • Build relationships across the team to understand how decisions flow and how to keep operations smooth

We're Seeking

  • Currently enrolled in a Master's program or MBA, available for a 6+ month internship (July 2026 - January 2027)

  • Previous experience in startups, venture capital, consulting, or investment banking—you've seen how high-performing organizations operate

  • Ability to ramp quickly and operate independently from day one—we need someone who can hit the ground running by September

  • Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail—you don't let things slip, and you're known for follow-through

  • Strong written and verbal communication—you can synthesize complex information for executives and investors

  • Comfortable working across all levels of an organization, from founders to individual contributors

  • Bias to action and ownership mentality—you see what needs to happen and make it happen without waiting for permission

  • Fluent in English; French is a plus but not required

Compensation and Benefits

  • Competitive compensation: €50,000 annual salary (prorated for 6-month contract)

  • Opportunity to work directly with founders at a Sequoia-backed startup

  • New MacBook Pro or Linux machine, monitor, keyboard, etc.

  • Beautiful office in the heart of Paris

  • Opportunity to travel to the US multiple times a year

  • Regular team events and offsite

Location

We're prioritizing building our team with an in-person culture at our offices in Paris, San Francisco, and New York because we value the magic that happens when talented people work closely together.

We have an office-first culture. Some of the best things about building at Dust are the energy, the fast decisions, and the unexpected conversations that unlock a hard problem, which happen because we are in the same room. Being together is not a formality, it is how we do our best work, and it is something we actively protect.

That said, we hire people with strong judgement and we extend that trust to how they manage their time. When working from home makes more sense for what you need to get done that day, we trust you to make that call.

Why Dust

The models are powerful enough. What's missing is the product layer where AI meets how companies actually work. That's what we're building: the infrastructure that lets any team turn scattered knowledge and tools into coordinated execution with agents they build, own, and run themselves.

We use Dust ourselves every day. We get to shape how humans and agents collaborate while solving our own problems with the product we ship. That loop is rare, and it's why we move fast.

If you're excited about defining a new category and want to join a determined team of optimists who focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously, we'd love to talk.

Even if you don't check every box in our requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value diverse perspectives and backgrounds, and we're more interested in your potential and passion than a perfect match to our checklist.

Learn how we think and work.

  • Our product constitution, a story about our mission

  • Agents at work - Latent Space, podcast with our cofounder, Stanislas Polu, 2024

  • LLMs reasoning and agentic capabilities over time - dotAI, podcast with our cofounder, Stanislas Polu, 2024

The Founder's Office at Dust exists to increase the operating leverage of the company during a period of hypergrowth.

This role is designed for high-agency problem solvers who want to work on the company's highest-priority initiatives. You'll operate across operations, GTM, recruiting, customer work, partnerships, and strategic projects, stepping into ambiguous situations and driving them forward with speed and judgment.

Some projects may last a day. Others may become entire functions.

You'll work closely with senior leadership, gain unusually high exposure to how a fast-growing AI company operates, and help scale the systems, processes, and decisions that matter most.

This is not a traditional strategy role, and it is not an assistant role. We are looking for people who thrive in ambiguity, move quickly, take ownership naturally, and enjoy building where structure does not yet exist.

What You'll Do

  • Own high-priority initiatives that do not yet have a clear long-term owner

  • Drive cross-functional projects across operations, GTM, recruiting, partnerships, and customer work

  • Identify operational bottlenecks and help resolve them quickly and effectively

  • Improve how the company executes by building lightweight systems, processes, and operating cadences

  • Help leadership translate priorities and decisions into execution

  • Step into ambiguous situations, create structure quickly, and drive momentum

  • Operate with high context across the company and connect dots across teams

  • Take on increasing scope and ownership as the company scales

Example Projects

Projects may include:

  • launching a new operational or GTM motion

  • supporting strategic customer initiatives

  • improving recruiting and interview operations

  • coordinating executive offsites or field events

  • driving follow-through on company priorities

  • building internal systems that improve execution speed and visibility

Requirements

Every candidate and employee's success is measured against the same 3 dimensions. Aptitude, Attitude and Agency.

Aptitude

  • You are highly analytical and structured in how you think

  • You can synthesize incomplete information quickly and identify what matters most

  • You communicate clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing

  • You learn new domains extremely quickly

  • You exercise strong judgment without requiring heavy oversight

  • You know how to balance speed and quality appropriately

Attitude

  • You are motivated by company-building, not narrowly defined ownership

  • You are energized by solving hard problems, especially when the path forward is unclear

  • You are equally comfortable thinking strategically and executing tactically

  • You have low ego and high curiosity

  • You build trust easily across technical and non-technical teams

  • You are comfortable operating in fast-moving and ambiguous environments

Agency

  • You proactively identify problems and move toward them

  • You take ownership without waiting for permission

  • You close loops and push work to completion

  • You are resourceful under constraints

  • You are comfortable making decisions with imperfect information

  • You naturally create momentum around you

Ideal Backgrounds

We care far more about slope, judgment, and ownership than any specific background, but strong candidates often come from environments like:

  • early-stage startups

  • Founder's Office or Chief of Staff roles

  • consulting paired with meaningful operator experience

  • venture capital or investing roles transitioning into operating

  • business operations or strategic operations

  • former founders or entrepreneurial builders

Why This Role Is Unique

  • Exposure to nearly every part of the company

  • Opportunity to work on the company's highest-priority initiatives

  • Extremely high learning velocity

  • Significant autonomy and ownership

  • A path toward larger operational or functional leadership roles over time

  • A front-row seat to scaling an AI company during a major platform shift

Compensation and Benefits

  • Competitive compensation: $175,000 - $200,000 base salary

  • 🏡 Substantial support for relocation (up to 10k€), including finding an apartment in San Francisco and supporting you with the visa procedure

  • Significant equity package in a Sequoia-backed startup

  • Health insurance for you and your dependents

  • New MacBook Pro or Linux machine, monitor, keyboard, etc.

  • Beautiful office in San Francisco

  • Opportunity to travel to Paris multiple times a year

  • Regular team events and offsite

Location

We're prioritizing building our team with an in-person culture at our offices in Paris, San Francisco, and New York because we value the magic that happens when talented people work closely together.

We have an office-first culture. Some of the best things about building at Dust are the energy, the fast decisions, and the unexpected conversations that unlock a hard problem, which happen because we are in the same room. Being together is not a formality, it is how we do our best work, and it is something we actively protect.

That said, we hire people with strong judgement and we extend that trust to how they manage their time. When working from home makes more sense for what you need to get done that day, we trust you to make that call.

Why Dust

The models are powerful enough. What's missing is the product layer where AI meets how companies actually work. That's what we're building: the infrastructure that lets any team turn scattered knowledge and tools into coordinated execution with agents they build, own, and run themselves.

We use Dust ourselves every day. We get to shape how humans and agents collaborate while solving our own problems with the product we ship. That loop is rare, and it's why we move fast.

If you're excited about defining a new category and want to join a determined team of optimists who focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously, we'd love to talk.

Even if you don't check every box in our requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value diverse perspectives and backgrounds, and we're more interested in your potential and passion than a perfect match to our checklist.

  • Agents at work - Latent Space, podcast with our cofounder, Stanislas Polu, 2024

  • LLMs reasoning and agentic capabilities over time - dotAI, podcast with our cofounder, Stanislas Polu, 2024

REQUIREMENTS

Every candidate and employee's success is measured against the same 3 dimensions. Aptitude, Attitude and Agency.

Aptitude

 - You are highly analytical and structured in how you think

 - You can synthesize incomplete information quickly and identify what matters most

 - You communicate clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing

 - You learn new domains extremely quickly

 - You exercise strong judgment without requiring heavy oversight

 - You know how to balance speed and quality appropriately

Attitude

 - You are motivated by company-building, not narrowly defined ownership

 - You are energized by solving hard problems, especially when the path forward is unclear

 - You are equally comfortable thinking strategically and executing tactically

 - You have low ego and high curiosity

 - You build trust easily across technical and non-technical teams

 - You are comfortable operating in fast-moving and ambiguous environments

Agency

 - You proactively identify problems and move toward them

 - You take ownership without waiting for permission

 - You close loops and push work to completion

 - You are resourceful under constraints

 - You are comfortable making decisions with imperfect information

 - You naturally create momentum around you

LOCATION

We're prioritizing building our team with an in-person culture at our offices in Paris, San Francisco, and New York because we value the magic that happens when talented people work closely together.

We have an office-first culture. Some of the best things about building at Dust are the energy, the fast decisions, and the unexpected conversations that unlock a hard problem, which happen because we are in the same room. Being together is not a formality, it is how we do our best work, and it is something we actively protect.

That said, we hire people with strong judgement and we extend that trust to how they manage their time. When working from home makes more sense for what you need to get done that day, we trust you to make that call.




WHY DUST

The models are powerful enough. What's missing is the product layer where AI meets how companies actually work. That's what we're building: the infrastructure that lets any team turn scattered knowledge and tools into coordinated execution with agents they build, own, and run themselves.

We use Dust ourselves every day. We get to shape how humans and agents collaborate while solving our own problems with the product we ship. That loop is rare, and it's why we move fast.

If you're excited about defining a new category and want to join a determined team of optimists who focus on users, ship fast, and don't take themselves too seriously, we'd love to talk.

Even if you don't check every box in our requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value diverse perspectives and backgrounds, and we're more interested in your potential and passion than a perfect match to our checklist.
